2604.26173.title: Entropy Centroids as Intrinsic Rewards for Test-Time Scaling
2604.26173.paper_summary: An effective way to scale up test-time compute of large language models is to sample multiple responses and then select the best one, as in Grok Heavy and Gemini Deep Think. Existing selection methods often rely on external reward models, which requires training a strong reward model and introduces additional computation overhead. As an alternative, previous approaches have explored intrinsic signals, such as confidence and entropy, but these signals are noisy with naive aggregation. In this work, we observe that high-entropy tokens tend to cluster into consecutive groups during inference, providing a more stable notion of model uncertainty than individual tokens. Together, these clusters reveal temporal patterns of model uncertainty throughout the inference process. Motivated by this observation, we propose to use the temporal structure of uncertainty as an intrinsic reward. To this end, we first formalize the basic unit of segment-level uncertainty as the High Entropy Phase (HEP), a variable-length segment that begins at a high-entropy token and ends when consecutive low-entropy tokens appear. We then define the Entropy Centroid, inspired by the concept of the center of mass in physics, as the weighted average position of all HEPs along the trajectory. Intuitively, a lower centroid indicates early exploration followed by confident generation, which we find often corresponds to higher response quality. Based on this insight, we propose the Lowest Centroid method, which selects the response with the lowest entropy centroid among multiple candidates. Experiments on mathematics, code generation, logical reasoning, and agentic tasks, across model scales ranging from 14B to 480B, show that Lowest Centroid consistently outperforms existing baselines and delivers stable gains as model size increases. Code is available at https://github.com/hkust-nlp/entropy-centroid.
2604.26173.paper_summary_zh: 有效地擴大大型語言模型的測試時間計算的一種方法是採樣多個回應，然後選擇最佳的一個，正如在Grok Heavy和Gemini Deep Think中所示。現有的選擇方法通常依賴於外部獎勵模型，這需要訓練一個強大的獎勵模型並引入額外的計算開銷。作為替代方案，之前的方法探索了內在信號，例如信心和熵，但這些信號在簡單聚合時會產生噪聲。在這項工作中，我們觀察到高熵標記在推理過程中傾向於聚集成連續的組，這提供了一種比單個標記更穩定的模型不確定性概念。這些聚類共同揭示了推理過程中模型不確定性的時間模式。受到這一觀察的啟發，我們提出使用不確定性的時間結構作為內在獎勵。為此，我們首先將段級不確定性的基本單位形式化為高熵階段（HEP），這是一個變長的段，從高熵標記開始，並在出現連續低熵標記時結束。我們然後定義熵質心，受物理學中質心概念的啟發，作為沿著軌跡所有HEP的加權平均位置。直觀上，較低的質心表示早期探索後的自信生成，我們發現這通常對應於更高的回應質量。基於這一見解，我們提出最低質心方法，該方法在多個候選者中選擇具有最低熵質心的回應。在數學、代碼生成、邏輯推理和代理任務上的實驗，涵蓋從14B到480B的模型規模，顯示最低質心方法始終優於現有基準，並隨著模型大小的增加而穩定增長。代碼可在https://github.com/hkust-nlp/entropy-centroid獲得。
2604.26173.author: Wenshuo Zhao et.al.
2604.26173.authors: Wenshuo Zhao, Qi Zhu, Xingshan Zeng, Fei Mi, Lifeng Shang, Yiren Feng
